title: Show geographic location data for existing video views

requirements.text: In the admin Video Management Panel (VideoManagementPanel.tsx), display geographic view breakdown data for videos that already have recorded views — not only for views captured after the feature was introduced. The country breakdown panel must show all view records currently stored in `videoViewRecords`, regardless of when they were recorded. If existing view records have no country data (e.g., country is empty or unknown), display them as 'Unknown' in the breakdown list.

quotes: se podra ver la ubicacion tambien de las visitas ya existentes?

acceptanceCriteria: The geographic breakdown panel in VideoManagementPanel.tsx renders for any video whose view records exist in the backend, including records created before geographic tracking was added.
View records with a missing, empty, or null country value are grouped and displayed under an 'Unknown' label in the breakdown panel.
The breakdown is sorted by view count descending, with 'Unknown' entries included in the sort.
No new views are required for the panel to appear — existing view records alone are sufficient to trigger display.
The panel remains visible only to admin users.

requirements.text: In the backend (main.mo), when returning per-video view records via the geographic view query function, do not filter out records that have an empty or missing country field. Ensure that all stored view records — including those recorded before country capture was implemented — are returned to the caller so the frontend can display them (grouping unknowns as 'Unknown').

quotes: se podra ver la ubicacion tambien de las visitas ya existentes?

acceptanceCriteria: The backend geographic view query returns all view records for a given video, including those with an empty or null country field.
Records with no country are returned with an empty string or a sentinel value that the frontend can detect and label as 'Unknown'.
No existing view records are dropped or hidden due to missing country data.

constraints: Do not modify files listed in frontend.immutablePaths.
All Motoko logic must remain in the single main actor (backend/main.mo).
The geographic breakdown panel must remain admin-only.
nonGoals: Retroactively resolving or inferring country data for existing views from IP logs or other metadata — unknown entries should simply be labeled 'Unknown'.
Adding new view-tracking or geolocation capture logic (already implemented in a prior feature).
